blocks:
Ayahs

وَٱلسَّمَآءِ وَٱلطَّارِقِ (۱)
By the heaven, and by the nightly visitor (1)
وَمَآ أَدْرَىٰكَ مَا ٱلطَّارِقُ (۲)
(and what will show you what is the nightly visitor? (2)
ٱلنَّجْمُ ٱلثَّاقِبُ (۳)
It is the brilliant star): (3)
إِن كُلُّ نَفْسٍ لَّمَّا عَلَيْهَا حَافِظٌ (٤)
there is a guard over every soul. (4)
فَلْيَنظُرِ ٱلْإِنسَـٰنُ مِمَّ خُلِقَ (٥)
So let man consider from what he was created. (5)
خُلِقَ مِن مَّآءٍ دَافِقٍ (٦)
He was created from an effusing fluid (6)
يَخْرُجُ مِنۢ بَيْنِ ٱلصُّلْبِ وَٱلتَّرَآئِبِ (۷)
which issues from between the loins and the breast-bones. (7)
إِنَّهُۥ عَلَىٰ رَجْعِهِۦ لَقَادِرٌ (۸)
Indeed He is able to bring him back [after death], (8)
يَوْمَ تُبْلَى ٱلسَّرَآئِرُ (۹)
on the day when the secrets are examined (9)
فَمَا لَهُۥ مِن قُوَّةٍ وَلَا نَاصِرٍ (۱٠)
and he shall have neither power nor helper. (10)

Key Points

Tariq means someone who enters at night and knocks on the door. Tarq is knocking, mitraqah is the tool for knocking (hammer), and tariq (path/road) is so called because it is trodden (knocked) underfoot. Thaqib means piercing, as if this star pierces the darkness of the night with its light. Sulb means firm (strong), and what is meant by it is the backbone (spinal column) of the back, while tara'ib refers to the bones of the chest. Tubla is derived from balwa, meaning test or trial, which is the factor that reveals truths. According to a narration from Imam Sadiq عليه السلام, the meaning of Tariq is the planet Saturn (Zuhal), which is the star of Amir al-Mu'minin and his successors (Awsiya'). (34) Ash'ath ibn Qays, who was among the hypocrites, brought a sweet dish (halwa) for Hadrat Ali عليه السلام at night and knocked on the door, intending to commit a transgression during the day by offering the gift of halwa at night. The Imam named him Tariq (the knocker) because he knocked on His door at night. (35) A verse similar to Ayah 4 of this Surah is found in Surah Al-Infitar: «و انّ علیكم لحافظین كراماً كاتبین یعلمون ما تفعلون» (Indeed, over you are guardians, noble writers, who know whatever you do.) Guardians have been appointed over you who nobly, without malice or suspicion, record your deeds and know what you do.

34) Tafsir Burhan. 35) Nahj al-Balaghah, Sermon 224.

Messages

1- Man and all his deeds are under the protection of Divine angels. (Both the recording of deeds and protection from accidents.) «ان كلّ نفس لمّا علیها حافظ»

2- The manner of a child's birth, from the formation of the sperm drop until emergence from the mother's womb, must be studied and scrutinized, as it provides a basis for both knowing Allah and knowing the Resurrection (Ma'ad). «فلینظر الانسان ممّ خلق...»

3- Man, due to possessing intellect, knowledge, and potential, must reflect upon his own creation. «فلینظر الانسان»

4- Attention to the creation of man is the basis for faith in the Resurrection. «على رجعه لقادر»

5- The Resurrection is physical, and man's body will be brought back to life on the Day of Judgment. «رجعه»

Explanations

Portrait of Surah At-Tariq

This Surah has seventeen verses and was revealed in Mecca. The name of the Surah is taken from the first verse and means 'the knocker' or 'the piercer'. The central themes of this Surah are two things: one, a warning regarding the Resurrection (Ma'ad), and the other, the blessing of the Quran and its high status. Drawing man's attention to his origin—which was a humble and insignificant fluid—prepares the ground for accepting Allah's eternal power to recreate man and His presence in the revealing court of the Day of Judgment. All this is derived from the truthful revelation of the heavens, which opponents consider a jest, while it is definite and certain, and whatever the disbelievers plot against it will return upon themselves.

Ayahs

وَٱلسَّمَآءِ ذَاتِ ٱلرَّجْعِ (۱۱)
By the resurgent heaven, (11)
وَٱلْأَرْضِ ذَاتِ ٱلصَّدْعِ (۱۲)
and by the furrowed earth: (12)
إِنَّهُۥ لَقَوْلٌ فَصْلٌ (۱۳)
it is indeed a conclusive discourse, (13)
وَمَا هُوَ بِٱلْهَزْلِ (۱٤)
and not a jest. (14)
إِنَّهُمْ يَكِيدُونَ كَيْدًا (۱٥)
Indeed they are devising a stratagem, (15)
وَأَكِيدُ كَيْدًا (۱٦)
and I [too] am devising a plan. (16)
فَمَهِّلِ ٱلْكَـٰفِرِينَ أَمْهِلْهُمْ رُوَيْدًۢا (۱۷)
So respite the faithless; give them a gentle respite. (17)

Key Points

"Raj‘" (رجع) means rain, perhaps because of the return (rujū‘) of water vapor to the earth in the form of rain. "Ṣad‘" (صَدع) means the splitting of the earth for the growth of plants. "Hazl" (هَزل) means meaningless speech. "Kayd" (كید) means a secret and surprising plot or scheme. "Mahhil" (مَهّل) and "Amhil" (اَمهِل) have the same meaning; they are presented in two forms for emphasis, and both mean to grant a respite (delay/time). "Ruwaydā" (رویدا) means a short respite. The sky and the earth are like the father and mother for the birth of plants, similar to the father and mother for the birth of humans. The descent of rain and the readiness of the earth are the foundation for the growth of plants. The revelation of the Qur’an and the readiness of man are likewise the basis for the growth of human beings. The name of the Qur’an is mentioned alongside the name of rain. To remove doubt, one must speak both logically and decisively. Allah’s successive oaths are meant to remove all doubt and uncertainty from the opponents and strengthen the belief and conviction of the believers. "Laqawli Faṣl" (لَقَوْلٌ فَصْلٌ) instead of "Laqawli Fāṣil" (لَقَوْلٌ فَاصِلٌ) means that the Qur’an is entirely decisive (faṣl) and separates truth from falsehood. Similar to when we say: So-and-so is sugar, meaning he is entirely sweetness. The Noble Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله said: Soon, tribulations will appear among you. They asked: What is the path to salvation? He replied: «كتاب اللّه فیه نبأ من قبلكم و خبر ما بعدكم و حكم ما بینكم هو الفصل» (The Book of Allah, in which is the news of those before you, the account of what comes after you, and the judgment concerning what is between you. It is the Decisive Word (al-Faṣl).) The Qur’an, which contains the news of the predecessors and the future generations and the judgment concerning you. It is the word that separates truth from falsehood. He further said: «و من ابتغى الهدى فى غیره اضلّه اللّه» Whoever seeks guidance in other than the Qur’an, Allah leads him astray. (36)

(36) Tafsir Namūneh.

Messages

  1. The Day of Judgment is the day when the thoughts and deeds of human beings are revealed and manifested. «یوم تبلى السرائر»
  2. Man's motives and inner dispositions affect his Day of Judgment. «یوم تبلى السرائر»
  3. The Day of Judgment is the day of the disclosure of secrets and shame: «تبلى السرائر», and man cannot do anything to conceal or compensate for his past. «فما له من قوّة و لا ناصر»
  4. The return (resurrection) of man on the Day of Judgment is like the return of evaporated seawater from the sky to the earth. «على رجعه لقادر - و السماء ذات الرجع»
  5. The value of every being depends on its effects and blessings. «ذات الرجع - ذات الصدع»
  6. The Qur’an is the Criterion (Furqān) and the separator of truth from falsehood, not a novel or unreal story. «انه لقول فصل و ما هو بالهزل»
  7. The deceit of the disbelievers is constant, severe, and certain; do not be heedless of it. «انهم یكیدون كیدا»
  8. Allah’s dealing with man is commensurate with his actions. If he steps onto the path of good, God guides him: «جاهدوا فینا لنهدینّهم» (37), and if he pursues plotting (kayd), he becomes entangled in the Divine plot. «یكیدون كیدا و اكید كیداً»
  9. The Qur’an is the standard for distinguishing truth from falsehood. «انّه لقول فصل»
  10. The disbelievers are opposing Allah, and Allah’s planning is severe. «یكیدون كیداً و اكید كیداً»
  11. They surprise the Muslims, and We (Allah) likewise surprise them. «یكیدون... اكید»
  12. When dealing with the enemy, neither rush yourselves nor ask Allah to rush. «فمهّل الكافرین»
  13. Never be heedless of the enemy, but granting them respite is permissible. «امهلهم»
  14. Broad-mindedness (patience/tolerance) is a necessary condition for leadership. «امهلهم»
  15. The amount of respite given to the enemy should not be so great that it takes the opportunity away from you. «رویدا»

(37) Al-‘Ankabūt, 69.
